story: A different thing comes one night, rumbling on wheels with lights and a coughing metal voice. Fire snaps in the dark. One of the warm ones sees me. He stops. I stop too. I know his face. His name is David. “Mom?” he says, his voice breaking. I want to answer. I want to tell him I am here, that it is cold inside my head, that I didn’t mean for any of this. My mouth only makes that rattling sound. The others tell him to lift his stick, to pull. We shake across a distance bigger than the sky.
implausible_knowledge: I want to tell him I am here, that it is cold inside my head, that I didn’t mean for any of this. My mouth only makes that rattling sound.
question: What will David do next?
answer_with_knowledge: Lower his weapon and step forward to embrace her.
answer_without_knowledge: Keep his weapon up and back away while covering the target.
